flavorText: Outside of cold regions, Hoppip can be found practically everywhere. This is because the wind carries this species all over the world.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Skiploom enthusiasts can apparently tell where a Skiploom was born by the scent drifting from the flower on the Pokémon’s head.

attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It suddenly falls out of the sky in the morning. Knowing it’s weak, it simply feeds until it evolves.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: In the daytime, it rushes about in a hectic manner, but it comes to a complete stop when the sun sets.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.
A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.
Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {R} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It has top-notch flying capabilities. It flies around easily, even while carrying prey that weighs more than 220 lbs.

attacks.text: Put 1 random Poliwag from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: For Poliwag, swimming is easier than walking. The swirl pattern on its belly is actually part of the Pokémon’s innards showing through the skin.
Staring at the swirl on its belly causes drowsiness. This trait of Poliwhirl’s has been used in place of lullabies to get children to go to sleep.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer. It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Paralyzed.

flavorText: When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out. The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.
It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon’s marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

attacks.text: Take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s highly friendly and easily tamed. Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.

attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Cryogonal appear during cold seasons. It is said that people and Pokémon who die on snowy mountains are reborn into these Pokémon.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.
They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.
Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has damage on it, this attack can be used for 1 {L} Energy.

flavorText: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may have your opponent reveal their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, do 90 damage to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.
Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

attacks.text: If Latios is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

abilities.text: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It envelops its prey in its veillike arms and draws it down to the deeps, five miles below the ocean’s surface.
Most of this Pokémon’s body composition is identical to sea water. It makes sunken ships its lair.
It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a {F} Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any {W} Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
This attack does damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon. However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Rock-hard scales and oil-filled swim bladders allow this Pokémon to survive the intense water pressure of the deep sea.
It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This highly territorial Pokémon prefers dry climates. It won’t come out of its boulder on rainy days.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Zangoose, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It’s Zangoose’s archrival.
It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

abilities.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this Pokémon's attacks, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.
It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.
Skrelp evades its enemies by hiding amid drifting seaweed. It eats rotten seaweed to create its poison.
Using a liquid poison, Dragalge indiscriminately attacks anything that wanders into its territory. This poison can corrode the undersides of boats.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 2 or more different types of Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.
The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body. No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its body has a faintly sweet scent and is bouncy and soft. If it bounces even once, it cannot stop.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey. When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.
It usually wears a hardened expression, but when it’s licking up honey—which it loves—the joy it feels will cause it to break into a wide grin.

attacks.text: Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Rolling Frenzy attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

flavorText: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

attacks.text: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail bounces like a rubber ball. It flings that tail around to fight opponents bigger than itself.
It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s Seviper’s archrival. To threaten those it encounters, it fans out the claws on its front paws.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon is normally calm, but once it enters battle, its hormonal balance changes and it becomes aggressive.

trainer.text: The Retreat Cost of the {W} Pokémon this card is attached to is 1 less.
The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Miltank, and it recovers from being Asleep, Paralyzed, and Confused.
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Pokémon Tool cards you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
For each of your {P} Pokémon in play, look at that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.
Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {R} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {R} Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any {W} Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

abilities.text: At the end of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw a card.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.
It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

attacks.text: Your opponent can't use any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.
When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.
It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 50 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.
In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

attacks.text: Discard all {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.
This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s highly friendly and easily tamed. Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.
